mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance at the 5% level in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive return frequency above 58% or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening corporate output capacity and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances during early-cycle periods or loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.35% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 65% of triggered months or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative return frequency above 55% or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal generates negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations or fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates contracting manufacturing activity and weakening corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The contraction signal fails to predict negative returns with a frequency above 60% or loses statistical significance during periods of stable monetary policy.
